candidates.name: Mark Kelly
candidates.nerdReason: Engineer and former NASA astronaut with an M.S. in Aeronautical Engineering. Demonstrated support for science capacity (e.g., NASA science funding) and a career built on technical and mission-driven decision systems.
education: B.S. Marine Engineering and Nautical Science, U.S. Merchant Marine Academy
M.S. Aeronautical Engineering, U.S. Naval Postgraduate School

candidates.name: Pete Buttigieg
candidates.nerdReason: Rhodes Scholar with a sustained record of technocratic governance themes (open data, modernization, measurable service delivery). Executive experience in infrastructure and transportation where performance management and safety data are central.
education: B.A. History and Literature, Harvard University
B.A. Philosophy, Politics and Economics, University of Oxford (Rhodes Scholar)

candidates.name: Josh Shapiro
candidates.nerdReason: Issued an executive order creating a values-based governance framework for generative AI (privacy, transparency, safety/security) and established an AI governing board—hallmarks of an expertise-forward, risk-managed approach to new technology.
education: B.A. Political Science, University of Rochester

candidates.name: Gavin Newsom
candidates.nerdReason: Expanded data-and-innovation capacity in California government (including a dedicated Office of Data and Innovation) and used executive orders and expert partnerships to study and operationalize responsible AI in state services.
education: B.S. Political Science, Santa Clara University

candidates.name: Kamala Harris
candidates.nerdReason: Strong record of climate-policy seriousness (major climate plan and Senate activity on climate/environmental justice) and a career that frequently frames climate as an evidence-driven, urgent governance problem.
education: J.D., University of California, Hastings College of the Law
experience: Vice President of the United States (2021–2025)

candidates.name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
candidates.nerdReason: Evidence of strong climate advocacy and an economics background, but less demonstrated track record of technocratic, expertise-led implementation (e.g., running large administrative systems with formal evaluation/governance structures) compared with higher-scoring contenders.
education: B.A. Economics and International Relations, Boston University

candidates.name: Mitt Romney
candidates.nerdReason: Harvard JD/MBA with a management consulting and Bain background, plus a long record of technocratic, expertise-oriented governance. In the Senate he engaged directly on science and technology policy, including NSF technology directorate legislation, semiconductor export-control oversight, and bipartisan AI-risk framework and legislation.
experience: President, Salt Lake Organizing Committee for the 2002 Winter Olympics

candidates.name: Nikki Haley
candidates.notNerdReason: Executive experience and an accounting background, but climate posture has been characterized as mixed—acknowledging climate risks/human role at times while supporting withdrawal from major climate agreements and rollbacks of clean-energy incentives.
education: B.S. Accounting and Finance, Clemson University
experience: U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations

candidates.name: Marco Rubio
candidates.notNerdReason: Public record includes rejecting mainstream scientific attribution of climate change to human activity, and State Department reorganization actions that reduced the footprint of climate-diplomacy offices and staff.

candidates.name: Ron DeSantis
candidates.notNerdReason: Florida has invested in resilience and environmental programs, but DeSantis-backed policy has de-emphasized climate change as a formal planning consideration and supported restrictions on net-zero style local climate programs—signals of politically constrained science alignment on climate.

candidates.name: JD Vance
candidates.notNerdReason: Public record includes skepticism about human-driven climate change and support for fossil-fuel expansion; has voiced COVID-19 vaccine skepticism in high-reach media; and promotes minimal AI regulation with less emphasis on safety governance than many expert frameworks.
experience: Vice President of the United States

candidates.name: Donald Trump Jr.
candidates.notNerdReason: Primarily a private-sector executive and political surrogate, with limited direct record of running evidence-based public programs or building expert-governance institutions compared with contenders who have held senior executive policymaking posts.
education: B.S. Finance and Real Estate, University of Pennsylvania (Wharton School)
experience: Executive Vice President, The Trump Organization

candidates.name: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
candidates.notNerdReason: Documented history of spreading inaccurate vaccine information and repeating debunked health claims; fact-checking reviews describe major parts of the MAHA agenda and related claims as not consistently evidence-based, which conflicts directly with expertise-led decision standards.
education: J.D., University of Virginia School of Law
experience: U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services
